mission.title: Install and Configure Kagenti
mission.description: Kagenti is a cloud-native middleware designed for deploying and orchestrating AI agents with a focus on security and scalability. It simplifies the deployment complexity and operational overhead associated with various AI frameworks by providing a standardized platform. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes and potential resource overhead for running the middleware.

steps.title: Add the Kagenti Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the Kagenti Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install Kagenti easily.

```bash

helm repo add kagenti https://kagenti.github.io/kagenti

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Kagenti repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install Kagenti CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Kagenti to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml

```

> This command applies the CRDs for Kagenti.

steps.title: Install Kagenti using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the Kagenti application in the specified namespace using Helm.

```bash

helm install kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --create-namespace --version 0.5.1

```

> Installs Kagenti in the 'kagenti' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to demonstrate how to use Kagenti.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

```

> Creates an example agent resource.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the created Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> Lists all KAgents in the 'kagenti' namespace.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Kagenti
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Kagenti installation and associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all associated data and resources.

```bash

helm uninstall kagenti --namespace kagenti

kubectl delete namespace kagenti

```

> Uninstalls the Kagenti release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Kagenti
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your resources. This step will upgrade Kagenti to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

helm upgrade kagenti kagenti/kagenti --namespace kagenti --version 0.5.1

kubectl get kagents -n kagenti

```

> Backup existing KAgent resources.
> Upgrades Kagenti to the specified version.
> Verifies that the KAgent resources are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kagents -n kagenti -o yaml > kagenti-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'KAgent not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified KAgent resource does not exist in the namespace.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the KAgent resource name is correct and that it exists in the 'kagenti' namespace.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image is not available or the image name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and ensure it is accessible from the Kubernetes cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** There may be an issue with the Helm chart or Kubernetes resources.

**Fix:**
Check the Helm release status with 'helm status kagenti --namespace kagenti' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) were not installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/kagenti/kagenti/v0.5.1/deploy/crds/kagenti.io_kagent_crd.yaml'.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced support for new agent types and improved security features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps are required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the KAgent resources listed with their statuses as 'Running' when everything is working correctly.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: kagenti.io/v1
kind: KAgent
metadata:
  name: example-agent
spec:
  image: registry.kagenti.io/example-agent:latest
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure your Kubernetes cluster meets the resource requirements and that you have the necessary tools installed.
